{"key_event":"Administrative centralization amid extractive expansion and depression","reasoning":"The 1926 and 1933 reorganizations, rail links, concessionary production, missions, taxation, and coerced labor deepened Belgian administrative and surveillance capacity, though the Depression limited fiscal strength and employment. Civil society remained extremely atomized: the Pende revolt and religious/literacy networks showed some capacity for resistance, but repression, indirect-rule chiefs, forced labor, and suppression of independent movements further narrowed autonomous organization.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":1.0,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":7.2}
